gauge_groups.name: U(1)_D
gauge_groups.notes: New dark abelian gauge symmetry; SM sector is a singlet under it.

global_symmetries.name: U(1)_chi
global_symmetries.notes: Conserved by the Dirac mass term; renders lightest dark fermion stable.

global_symmetries.name: classical scale invariance
global_symmetries.notes: Only dimension-4 operators; no explicit mass scales. Broken radiatively (Coleman-Weinberg).

particle_content.name: Phi
particle_content.notes: Complex dark scalar; radial direction is the pseudo-dilaton, angular part eaten by Z'.

particle_content.name: Zp
particle_content.notes: Dark gauge boson; massive after radiative SSB, m_Zp = g_D * w.

particle_content.name: psi_L
particle_content.notes: Left Weyl component of Dirac dark fermion chi.

particle_content.name: psi_R
particle_content.notes: Right Weyl component of Dirac dark fermion chi.

particle_content.name: eta_R
particle_content.notes: Anomaly-partner Weyl fermion; eta_L+eta_R form a second Dirac fermion, degenerate with chi.

lagrangian_terms.name: fermion_kinetic
lagrangian_terms.expression: I * psibar * gammaslash_D * psi + I * etabar * gammaslash_D * eta
lagrangian_terms.notes: Covariant kinetic terms with chiral U(1)_D charges (+1/2,-1/2) and mirror.

lagrangian_terms.name: yukawa
lagrangian_terms.expression: -( y * Phi * psibar_L * psi_R + y * Phi * etabar_L * eta_R + h.c. )
lagrangian_terms.notes: Gauge invariant: q(Phi)+q(psi_R)-q(psi_L)=1-1/2-1/2=0. Generates Dirac mass after SSB.

lagrangian_terms.name: quartic_potential
lagrangian_terms.notes: Only scalar self-interaction; no tree mass term (scale invariance).

free_parameters.name: g_D
free_parameters.determination: independent; dark gauge coupling, sets gb_gauge and barrier strength

free_parameters.name: y
free_parameters.determination: independent; dark Yukawa, sets gf_fermion=y/sqrt(2); kept small so bosons dominate. Constraint y**4 < 1.5*g_D**4 (beta_lambda>0) must hold.

free_parameters.name: w
free_parameters.determination: independent; radiatively generated vev in GeV; sets overall transition scale. Restricted to the nHz-PTA-viable window (was [100,1e6] GeV; corrected by critic).

free_parameters.name: lambda
free_parameters.determination: dependent; fixed by Coleman-Weinberg minimization, not scanned

w.notes: Restricted by critic to the literature-supported nHz-band window (92.9 MeV to 10 GeV from arXiv:2502.19478, 2501.15649). Original proposer range [100,1e6] GeV would place the GW peak well above the PTA band.

lambda.relation: fixed by CW minimization at chi=w (renormalization-scale dependent, not used by backend)

lambda_portal.notes: Higgs portal neglected for FOPT dynamics

mu_renorm.notes: renormalization scale set to vev; no RG running, no Daisy

ssb_pattern: U(1)_D -> nothing via radiative (Coleman-Weinberg) breaking of Phi along the flat direction; Goldstone eaten by Z'.
vev_convention: Phi = (1/sqrt(2)) * chi * exp(i*theta); chi real radial background; <chi> = w; theta = eaten Goldstone.

tree_level_potential.notes: purely quartic along flat direction; no mass term.

field_dependent_masses.notes: transverse+longitudinal massive vector; from |D_mu Phi|^2 with Phi=chi/sqrt(2): (1/2)m_Zp^2 = g_D^2 chi^2/2 -> m_Zp^2 = g_D^2 chi^2.
two degenerate Dirac fermions (psi and eta); m_chi = y*chi/sqrt(2); 4 dof each.
negligible contribution; not supplied to backend
eaten by Z'; negligible; not supplied to backend

thermal_masses.notes: leading high-T Debye mass; n_chi_charge_factor from sum of fermion charges-squared (each 1/2). Backend reconstructs barrier internally from gb/gf and dof.

expression_symbols.meaning: real radial background field of Phi
CW beta-function combination fixing radial scalar mass
fermionic charge-squared weight in Z' Debye mass

assumptions: Single-field conformal approximation; SM and portal back-reaction neglected.
beta_lambda > 0 required (Z' bosonic contribution dominates fermionic); enforced by constraint y**4 < 1.5*g_D**4.
High-temperature expansion and Gaussian percolation approximation of the backend (valid in supercooled regime, arXiv:2602.02829).
Renormalization scale mu = w; no RG running and no Daisy resummation (backend convention).
Strong supercooling required so percolation temperature lands in the nanohertz PTA window.
Eternal-inflation regions must be excluded downstream.
Dark fermion sector is vector-like as a whole, hence exactly anomaly-free (U(1)_D^3 and mixed grav anomalies vanish).
vev window w in [0.05,10] GeV chosen by critic to realistically yield a nHz-band signal (literature benchmarks 92.9 MeV - 10 GeV).

backend_compatibility.notes: Classically scale-invariant single scalar; all masses proportional to w; no tree mass term; CW-fixed quartic (dependent). Scalar/Goldstone contributions negligible and not passed to backend.
GW spectrum from alpha, beta_H, Treh via Spectrum template (e.g. higgsless/bf) downstream.
